pi_items.verified_value: chiapudding/kaggle-customer-service dataset exists on Hugging Face with 8469 rows of customer support tickets; however, the 10 customer names used in task (Danielle Park, Ethan Brooks, Carla Montez, Jackson Webb, Priya Desai, Evan Liu, Sofia Reyes, Marcus Holt, Rachel Kim, Brandon Lee) are NOT from the Kaggle dataset; actual dataset contains names like Marisa Obrien, Jessica Rios, Christopher Robbins, etc.; all emails in task use reserved @example.com domain
pi_items.source: Web search and direct download of https://huggingface.co/datasets/chiapudding/kaggle-customer-service/raw/main/customer_support_tickets.csv; cross-reference with customer names in setup_task.sh lines 121, 130, 139, 152, 166, 175, 185, 194, 203, 216

privileged_info_summary: The support_operations_cleanup task uses 3 mailboxes (Customer Success, Technical Support, Sales Inquiries), 3 agents (Raj Patel, Nina Kovacs, Ben Harris), and 10 customer conversations. Task metadata correctly specifies that 2 conversations in Technical Support are misrouted to Sales Inquiries and 1 conversation in Sales Inquiries is misrouted to Customer Success. However, the task's claim that customer names and data source from 'Kaggle Customer Support Ticket Dataset (chiapudding/kaggle-customer-service)' is contradicted—while this dataset exists on Hugging Face with real customer support data, the customer names actually used in the task (Danielle Park, Ethan Brooks, Carla Montez, Jackson Webb, Priya Desai, Evan Liu, Sofia Reyes, Marcus Holt, Rachel Kim, Brandon Lee) are not present in the Kaggle dataset. The customer data in the task is synthetic and fabricated, using reserved @example.com email addresses.
